summary:
Claim: The cricothyroid muscle is the only laryngeal muscle not supplied by the recurrent laryngeal nerve.  
Evidence: - The cricothyroid muscle receives its motor innervation from the superior laryngeal nerve. - All other major laryngeal muscles (e.g., cricohyoid, arytenoid, aryepiglotticus) are innervated by the recurrent laryngeal nerve.  
Answer: B

Answer: B. Answer choice: B
